Open TownSq Contact the Board

Welcome home,
neighbor. 👋

Armstrong Village is a townhome community in Upper Marlboro, Maryland where neighbors know each other, look out for one another, and make this place better together every day.

What's Coming Up Open TownSq
🏡 292 homes
🎉 Community events & gatherings
🏡 Maintained common areas
🤝 Active homeowner board
📍 Westphalia, MD

What's happening in the village

From movie nights to morning walks to the big Community Day bash - there's always something worth showing up for.

Community Day

Community Day 2026

Our biggest neighborhood celebration of the year - food, activities, music, and time with the people who live next door. Details coming this summer.

📅 Saturday, August 29
Volunteer or suggest ideas →
Coming Up

Outdoor Movie Night

Bring a blanket, grab a spot, and enjoy a family-friendly film outside together. Gather at 7:30 PM, movie starts at 8:30 PM.

📅 Friday, July 25
Details on TownSq →
Annual Meeting

Community Meeting

Open to all residents. Hear board updates, ask questions, and vote in the director election for the Member-at-Large seat.

📅 Wednesday, July 23 · 6:30 PM · Zoom
View on TownSq →
Every Saturday

Morning Walk & Run Club

Show up, say hello, go at your own pace. Walkers and runners both welcome - no sign-up, no commitment, just good neighbors.

📅 Every Saturday morning · May through September
Holiday Fun

Halloween Decorating Contest

Deck out your home and let the board be the judge. Small gift card prize for the winner. Low pressure, high fun.

📅 Judged Saturday, October 25
Holiday Fun

Holiday Lights Contest

Same spirit as Halloween - decorate, compete, win. Announced November 15 so you have time to plan something great.

📅 Judged Saturday, December 13

For the latest news, check the Armstrong Village Facebook group or TownSq.

Community Day 2025

A neighborhood that shows up

Real moments from last year's Community Day: neighbors volunteering, welcoming one another, sharing food, and catching up in the common area.

Be a good neighbor

Small things add up. Here's how you can help make Armstrong Village even better.

🌳

Help with Community Day

Every year we throw a big day for the neighborhood. We need people to help plan, set up, cook, and clean up.

🛠️

Volunteer projects

Occasional work days for common areas, landscaping touch-ups, or helping neighbors who need a hand.

📣

Join a committee

ARC, social, beautification, or communications. If you have ideas or time, we'd love to have you.

💬

Get on TownSq

Stay connected with neighbors, access documents, and keep up with everything happening in the community.

Talk to the Board about getting involved

Helpful things for residents

Everything you need to keep your home and our neighborhood in good shape.

Snow Removal & Emergency Contacts +

Snow & Ice Removal
Level Green Landscaping manages snow and ice removal for Armstrong Village common areas and private roads, effective August 1, 2026.
Snow service issues: corey.rill@levelgreenlandscaping.com

Property Management — CMC/Associa
Eddie Harrison, Portfolio Manager
eddie.harrison@cmc-management.com

Board of Directors

Police (non-emergency): Prince George's County — 301-336-1212

Governing Documents & Bylaws +

Full governing documents are available on TownSq. Key documents include:

  • Declaration of Covenants, Conditions & Restrictions (CCRs)
  • Association Bylaws
  • Amendments 1, 3, 4 & 6 (including WestEnd annexation)
  • Parking, Towing & CCR Enforcement Policies

Life in Armstrong Village

Tree-lined streets, well-kept common areas, and familiar places from a little higher up.

Our Community is Growing

Armstrong Village is expanding with WestEnd — 88 new townhomes being built by Pulte Home Company on an adjacent parcel to the west. When complete, our combined community will total 292 units.

WestEnd was formally annexed into Armstrong Village HOA in 2024. Each home that closes adds to the Association's assessment income and expands the community we all share.

The board actively monitors construction progress and works with Pulte to ensure the transition of common areas and roads is handled properly for all residents.

WestEnd at a Glance

HOA Annexation

New WestEnd homeowners are automatically members of Armstrong Village HOA.
